title: Is Everyone Hanging Out Without Me? (And Other Concerns)

chapters.title: Networking works wonders
chapters.text:
Mindy Kaling had fun learning Latin in high school, watching movies with her girlfriends, experiencing long crushes on older guys who didn't know her, and hanging out with her family. In her early years, she was not a big star, mainly because she was often overlooked at school; hence, her achievements had nothing to do with her upbringing or popularity in high school.

Mindy never attended a single party with alcohol, and nobody ever passed her marijuana. At the age of 16, she discovered that marijuana and pot were identical, not through friends, but from watching a rerun of the 2012 comedy 21 Jump Street. Her parents didn't allow her any social activities during the week because those days were for homework. Mindy could watch The X-Files on Friday nights as a reward if she behaved herself.

She watched Seinfeld on Thursday nights on several occasions, but only if she had performed exceptionally well in school tests. This restriction upset her, as the glamorous and carefree high school life portrayed in TV series and movies was not a reality for her.

Hard work and dedication can lead you to success, even if it means sacrificing some youthful pleasures.

As this summary unfolds, you will glimpse into Mindy Kaling's life — her romances and female friendships, her teenage experience of bullying, and her path to Hollywood success. Ready to be inspired to follow your dreams? Then dive right in!

chapters.title: Children can be cruel, too
chapters.text:
Mindy Kaling has always been chubby — except the day she was born when she weighed 6 pounds. When she was in first grade, she weighed 68 pounds. She was 14 when she first attempted to lose weight and 19 when she tried again. At 14, she lost weight because of Daunte Diallo, a guy from Senegal. Diallo was a popular kid on the basketball team, loved by both teachers and students. But Diallo bullied Kaling verbally for being overweight. He said she could be beautiful if she lost weight and later started calling her a whale, forcing her to focus on her diet.

Bullies have no code of conduct. ~ Mindy Kaling

Because it was the pre-Internet era, Mindy had limited resources for weight loss. Therefore, she devised her diet plan, which involved eating only half of her usual portions and skipping desserts. Without any exercise, this approach led to a loss of 30 pounds in two months. Her motivation for losing weight was to end Diallo's mockery and to be able to hang out in the freshmen center once more. Yet, Diallo's bullying continued.

Always be kind to people; you don’t know the battles they face.

One day, while walking to her class, Kaling met Diallo and his friends in the hallway. He mocked, "Remember when Mindy was like a whale?" while puffing out his cheeks to mimic being overweight. His friends found it amusing, but Kaling did not. Luckily, Diallo left school during his junior year.

Mindy Kaling's weight loss at 19 was significant because that was when she started exercising. In 1999, at Dartmouth College, her best friend, Brenda Withers, taught her how to run. Their workout routine involved walking for 20 minutes and short bursts of running between lamp posts or street signs. Kaling lost 30 pounds in a semester. After that, if someone called her chubby, it no longer kept her up at night.

chapters.title: The first steps into adult life
chapters.text:
Mindy believes that teenage girls shouldn't worry about being super popular or the best actress or athlete in high school. After graduation, people typically do not care about anything that seems vital during school years. Ambitious teenagers should be aware that it is okay to be quiet. Besides being a great delight to your parents, there’s always time to figure out what you want in the future.

To make giant strides in life, you must focus on your goals, not the past.

In July 2001, Kaling moved to New York with her friends, Brenda and Jocelyn. Unemployed, they shared a railroad-style apartment in Windsor Terrace, Brooklyn. She went for several auditions in New York but was unsuccessful.

In October 2001, at age 22, Mindy finally got a job babysitting two adorable girls, Dylan and Haley. She was an excellent babysitter because she had an open mind concerning matters the girls wanted to discuss. Babysitting for her was about caring for the young and eating their food.

chapters.title: Brenda and Mindy turn into Matt and Ben
chapters.text:
Kaling continued her babysitting job and was finally able to pay her bills. But she and Brenda, who worked as a public school substitute teacher, needed to do something creative. No one was hiring them as actresses and screenwriters; therefore, they opted to create their own acting and writing opportunities. They set aside one hour daily for writing. But unfortunately, they didn't make great use of this time, often lying on the sofa watching reality shows.

Gradually, they started creating skits in which they impersonated characters, practicing them during commutes or while preparing to go out. A favorite routine involved Withers copying Matt Damon while Kaling took on the role of Ben Affleck. Damon and Affleck were popular actors in the early 2000s and featured in the cinematic drama Good Will Hunting. From this, the girls wrote a play, submitting it to the New York International Fringe Festival.

If you see no opportunities for growth, craft them for yourself.

Their first Matt & Ben show had no directors or characters because they couldn't afford them. In 2002, the Fringe Festival recognized their play as the best out of 500 entries. The New Yorker described it as goofy, funny, and surprisingly credible. The magazine stated Kaling and Withers had produced one of the finest male-bonding narratives since Damon and Pythias or Oscar and Felix.

Life for Mindy Kaling and Brenda Withers began to shift. Producers reached out to move the play to an off-Broadway stage. They got a director and had a budget. The show's duration was short enough for two performances each evening. Famous actors and actresses came to see the play, which helped their sales so much that they added a third performance per night.

chapters.title: Boy vs. man mentality
chapters.text:
Mindy Kaling's first date was at age 25 with Peter Parker, a comedy writer, and one date turned into four. Parker had a quaint Spanish ranch-style house in Hollywood. He was Kaling's first boyfriend who indeed turned his house into a home. Things didn’t work out, but she observed in Parker the quality she would later consider one of the most important — not being afraid of commitment. Commitment to Mindy isn’t only about romantic relationships; it's a commitment to figuring out life.

Until Kaling was 30, she dated boys instead of men because men were scary to her. Men know what they want, make concrete plans, and own alarm clocks. They sleep on a mattress that isn't on the floor, tip generously, and go to the dentist. Men purchase fresh shampoo instead of adding water to a nearly finished bottle. They initiate kisses without a long preface about their kissing intentions.

Boys are charming. They bring a backpack to work and have a lovely way of trailing off their sentences. Boys choose to have their haircuts done by their roommates and can swiftly pack their lives into a gym bag to relocate to Brooklyn for a job opportunity. Typically, they're short of money, but when they have cash, they'll splurge on a trip to Colorado for a music festival. Boys often struggle to adjust their tone when switching from chatting with friends to speaking with parents. They treat their parents like buddies and often let them foot the bill during dinners. Boys' irregular work schedules mean they're up for lengthy chats in a diner at 3 am. However, dating them becomes challenging after you're 30.

Maturity is the crux of stable and healthy romantic relationships.

chapters.title: The unexpected place of power
chapters.text:
During the notorious 2007 writers' strike, Mindy Kaling purchased a Spanish-style residence in a Los Angeles neighborhood. While Kaling has a cozy office in her home, she prefers working in bed. Mindy jokes that when she writes, she looks like she's recovering from an illness with her laptop sitting atop a blanket or a Notre Dame hoodie. She predominantly writes at home, without music on, to maintain focus.

I’m kind of a mess. I did, however, fulfill a childhood dream of writing and acting in television and movies. ~ Mindy Kaling

Mindy Kaling enjoyed working with the writing staff because of the social nature of the job. She is a very talkative and social person. Kaling's office at work was convenient, though it mainly served as a cluttered storage space. The Internet poses a significant distraction — one quick search on almond milk production can turn into hours of reading about the Donner Party and messaging friends. Kaling's ratio of productive writing to procrastination stands at 1:7. In an eight-hour writing day, only one hour is genuinely productive. She spends the remaining time in pre-writing rituals: wandering the house, flattening recycling boxes, enjoying DVD extras from the BBC's Pride & Prejudice, arranging snacks for a writing session, and watching videos of toddlers mastering the 'Single Ladies' dance.

Even the most talented people struggle with procrastination; don’t be hard on yourself for getting distracted.

chapters.title: Networking works wonders
chapters.text:
Mindy Kaling attributes her Hollywood success to screenwriter Greg Daniels and his wife, who recognized her talent after seeing her perform in Matt & Ben. A week later, Kaling was hired as a staff writer for the 2005-2013 NBC television series The Office.

Being a staff writer was very stressful for Mindy at the beginning. Although funny, she was inexperienced in a space full of seasoned comic writers. She was concerned Greg Daniels would notice this talent inequity and fire her. Gradually, as with most colleagues you spend a lot of time with, Kaling formed friendships on set. The lead writers in the first season were Greg Daniels, Paul Lieberstein, Mike Schur, B. J. Novak, and Mindy Kaling herself, with Larry Wilmore and Lester Lewis contributing as consultants for three days a week. They shot The Office at a secluded end of a street in an industrial area of Panorama City in the San Fernando Valley. The series gained critical acclaim during the second season due to exceptional episodes.

Success often comes from not only individual talent but also the support and guidance of others.

Later, Kaling wrote for Saturday Night Live (SNL), a late-night American comedy show. Recommended by her colleague Mike Schur, she joined during the break between The Office's second and third seasons. Among the SNL writers were Amy Poehler, Seth Meyers, Rachel Dratch, and Tina Fey, who often worked alone or in pairs. Because Mindy didn't know anybody on the team, she felt hesitant to approach her colleagues and pitch her ideas.

chapters.title: How to sneak out of any social gathering
chapters.text:
Mindy Kaling never had a one-night stand and didn't even know what hooking up meant. But she loves “Irish exits” — when you leave a party without telling anyone.

Kaling's exit style involves a bit of trickery. She often loudly asks, “Where's the bathroom?” while mimicking the gestures of a confused tourist. However, instead of heading to the restroom, she picks up her coat and slips out. Alternatively, she might claim she's left her car lights on or forgot to lock it. Cars serve as excellent excuses for quick exits. No one questions car troubles because they're inherently dull to hear about. Mindy chooses to exit this way, not because she is too busy or cool for social rituals, but at events with over 30 people, she prefers to skip the long process of hellos and goodbyes.

It's okay to set boundaries and prioritize your own needs as long as you're respectful toward others.

chapters.title: Mindy’s take on marriage
chapters.text:
Shakespeare believed the journey leading up to marriage was more entertaining than married life, so he often ended his works with wedding ceremonies. As a child, Mindy Kaling observed that approximately 25% of her peers had divorced parents. As an adult, she has encountered many people who are either unhappily married or divorced. Mindy's parents get along well because they share common interests. They can endlessly discuss the same topics, which leads to a harmonious relationship.

Their shared passions include discussing rose bushes, mulch, and shrub placement. They can spend hours together, endlessly chatting about rhododendrons and the TV show Men of a Certain Age, watching Piers Morgan, enjoying a vanilla milkshake, and then heading to bed.

Marriage is a beautiful union, but it requires effort and hard work.

Despite popular opinions, Mindy Kaling believes that marriage shouldn't be complicated. Married people must strive to keep their relationship intact despite the challenges because it's a privilege to be married. Many people desire marriage but are unlucky with finding a good partner, and many envy what married couples have.

Mindy believes e very happily married person should be grateful for their relationship because they’re the star of a piece of Shakespearean work, wearing flowers in their hair at the end of the play.

Did you know? Mike Schur supplied Mindy Kaling with a eulogy in advance of her death.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Mindy Kaling is 42 with two kids — Katherine and Spencer. She is an actress and a producer and still calls herself chubby. She admits to doing a lot of yoga, walking, and jogging during pregnancy to lose her baby weight. She works out four to five times a week while not filming and currently lives in Los Angeles. In 2020, Kaling and Lang Fisher created Never Have I Ever, a comedy-drama series about a first-generation Indian-American teenager’s complicated life. The comedy is partially based on Kaling’s experience as an Indian-American teen coming of age in high school.

She is also part of the cast of Monster at Work, a Disney series released in June 2021. Monster at Work is an animated comedy series about mechanic Tylor Tuskmon’s desire to get to the Laugh Floor at Monsters Inc. Kaling voiced Val Little, an ardent member of the Monsters, Inc. Facilities Team (MIFT). She is set to co-write the script for the third installment in the Legally Blonde series with Dan Goor. Mindy is also working on another workplace comedy involving the 17-time NBA champion basketball team — the Los Angeles Lakers. This untitled series will center on the personal and professional dynamic between the family owners and the team that collectively runs the sporting franchise.

Try this


• Focus on your passion: Keep sight of it, even if you have to try other things temporarily.
• Discuss your ideas with your friends: They may give you ideas on how to make them better.
• Join a community of people who share similar interests: It will go a long way toward fueling your passion.
